section0.title: From 3D to real-world systems.
section0.body: I started my journey in CGI and 3D, developing a strong visual and problem-solving mindset. Over the years I transitioned into web development and later into IT support and systems integration. Today I work in a structured manufacturing environment, dealing with internal tools, automation, troubleshooting and business-critical workflows.

section1.title: Systems, integrations and AI.
section1.body: Over the last few years I've worked on ERP/PLM integrations, APIs, scripting, Linux environments, automation and AI-assisted workflows. I'm particularly interested in the practical integration of AI into production and operational processes, with a focus on efficiency, reliability, security and system control.

bio: I work as a Web Developer and IT Specialist in an enterprise manufacturing company within the luxury fashion industry. My work focuses on internal development, systems integration, IT support and operational process automation.
Over the last few years I've worked on ERP and PLM integrations, APIs, scripting, Linux environments, infrastructure troubleshooting and business-critical tools used daily in production.
I work daily across Windows and Linux environments, supporting infrastructure, users and operational systems in complex production contexts.
My background started in CGI and 3D, an experience that still influences how I approach design, logic and problem solving.
Today my focus is building reliable systems, automating real-world workflows and integrating AI tools in a pragmatic and sustainable way.
about.aiTitle: AI as a tool, not a shortcut.
aiBody: I closely follow the evolution of AI and actively integrate it into my workflows in practical ways. I use AI tools for development, automation, research and operational support, while maintaining strong attention to validation, security and system understanding.
I believe the real value isn't in using AI blindly, but in knowing when to use it, how to validate its output and how to integrate it into real workflows without creating unnecessary dependencies.

work.cta: Projects, systems and tools built for real-world environments.
